title: Cavity wall insulation: when to fill and when to leave it
subtitle: Exposure zones, cavity width, and wall condition determine whether fill is appropriate
excerpt: Cavity wall fill reduces a 1.5 W/m²·K unfilled cavity to 0.4-0.5 W/m²·K at a typical cost of installed. But in exposed coastal zones or on walls with existing damp, fill can drive moisture through the wall. This guide covers the checks to make before proceeding.
sourceNotes: CIGA (Cavity Insulation Guarantee Agency), BBA/KIWA cavity fill eligibility criteria and exposure zone maps. BRE BR 443 cavity wall U-value reference values. EST cavity wall insulation guidance.
aliases: cavity wall insulation should I do it

glossaryTerms.definition: Thermal transmittance. The watts of heat that flow through one square metre of building fabric per degree of temperature difference (W/m²·K). Lower is better. Unfilled cavity wall: ~1.5; filled cavity wall: ~0.4–0.5.
A localised gap or break in the insulation envelope where heat short-circuits the resistance — wall ties through a cavity, a window reveal, a steel lintel.

content.text: A standard 50 mm unfilled cavity wall has a
U-value

around 1.5 W/m²·K. Fill with blown mineral wool or EPS beads and that drops to 0.4-0.5 W/m²·K. For a typical semi-detached, that is a saving of 6,000-8,000 kWh per year, or around at current gas prices. The work takes half a day and is carried out entirely from outside via a drill-and-inject process.
When fill is appropriate
Cavity width 50 mm or more, narrower cavities can be filled but require a different product (typically injected foam).
Cavity built after around 1920, earlier construction more often has no clear cavity or a rubble-filled void.
Wall in CIGA exposure zone 1 or 2 (sheltered or moderate), zone maps are available from CIGA's website.
No existing damp problem in the wall.
No existing fill already in the cavity (a borescope survey confirms this).
When not to fill
CIGA exposure zone 3 or 4 (severe or very severe), coastal Scotland, West Wales, exposed upland England. Fill in these zones can wick rain water across the cavity.
Existing damp penetration through the outer leaf, fill will not cure damp and may worsen it.
Defective or missing wall ties, a borescope survey identifies these; they must be repaired before fill.
Single-leaf or solid wall construction, there is no cavity to fill.
Fill types
Blown mineral wool, most common, reversible (can be extracted), CIGA-guaranteed. Lambda 0.034 W/m·K.
EPS (expanded polystyrene) beads, bonded with adhesive so they cannot slump. Lambda 0.036 W/m·K. Used where extraction may be needed (e.g. for future window replacement).
Polyurethane foam, injected as liquid, sets solid. Best thermal performance but not reversible.
Thermal bridges after filling
Even after fill, wall ties, window reveals, and lintels form
thermal bridges
that reduce the effective whole-wall U-value. The BRE correction for standard metal ties in a mineral wool-filled cavity is approximately +0.02 W/m²·K, small but not zero.
Getting a survey

CIGA-registered installers carry out a borescope survey before filling, a small hole is drilled and a camera inspects the cavity for width, existing fill, debris, and tie condition. This survey is typically included in the installation quote. Check the installer holds a current CIGA registration before booking.
